data.title: Repsol 4Q Adj Income EUR643M
SBM Offshore sees slower 2025 growth after record-high yearly revenue
data.description:
Dutch oil and gas services company SBM Offshore said on Thursday it sees slower growth in 2025 after posting a slight revenue beat this year, driven by the sale of its floating production, storage and offloading vessels (FPSO) in Suriname and Guyana.

The Amsterdam-based group, which provides floating production solutions to the offshore energy industry, said it sees 2025 directional revenue guidance above $4.9 billion, with around $2.7 billion from its turnkey business, which builds and sells the FPSO vessels.

The company sees 2025 directional EBITDA at $1.55 billion.

"The debottlenecking of Guyanese FPSOs safely brought all three units, Liza Destiny, Liza Unity and Prosperity, to oil production levels beyond their original investment basis," the company said in a statement.

SBM Offshore calculates its results using a method of "directional reporting" that records payments made during the construction phase and prior to a lease's execution as revenue.

SBM Offshore reported directional revenue at a record high level of $6.1 billion for 2024, compared to $4.53 billion in 2023. That slightly exceeded its guidance of $6.0 billion.

data.title: Gold and yen bask in the limelight
data.description:
GOLD AND YEN BASK IN THE LIMELIGHT

A bevy of tariff headlines this week along with geopolitical worries has left investors wary and weary, taking stocks lower in Asian hours, gold to a record peak and the yen to its highest in over two months as sentiment remains fragile.

European stocks are poised to tread with caution on Thursday, futures indicate, after the pan-European STOXX 600 index dropped nearly 1% in the previous session, its biggest daily drop in two months.

Bear in mind, the benchmark index, along with other European bourses, has had a strong start to the year, clocking in a double-digit rise so far in 2025, far outperforming U.S. stocks.

Markets have sort of grown accustomed to the various tariff vows from U.S. President Donald Trump, with many analysts seeing the threats as an opening gambit in long-drawn-out negotiations with foes and friends alike.

But Trump denouncing Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy as a "dictator" and warning he had to move quickly to secure peace or risk losing his country brought to the fore the ample worries around geopolitics, sapping risk sentiment.

The risk-off mood meant the yen - already underpinned by rising odds of the Bank of Japan hiking rates again - was the main mover among currencies, hitting its highest level since early December and was last at 150.48 per dollar.

Gold prices hit yet another record high on safe-haven flows, taking its 2025 gains to 12%. For those keeping track, that's the ninth time the metal has touched an all-time high this year. And that comes after a 27% rise last year, its strongest annual performance in over a decade.

Both Citi and Goldman raised their target price on gold this month, predicting it to breach the $3,000 mark. A large part of the reason behind the bullishness is sustained demand from central banks. Perhaps in these uncertain times, gold is all that shines.

Focus will also be on earnings from Mercedes-Benz and Renault as the European automakers try and allay investor fears on tariffs.

Key developments that could influence markets on Thursday:

- Germany producer prices for January

- Earnings: Airbus, Lloyds Banking, Mercedes-Benz and Renault

(Ankur Banerjee)

*****

data.title: LSEG's Shares Poised to Perform Well — Market Talk
data.description: London Stock Exchange Group's shares are set up well to perform this year, RBC Capital Markets analysts Ben Bathurst and Jude Neanor write in a note. The stock-exchange and financial-information company is expected to deliver sustained strong underlying earnings and free cash-flow growth, supported by recent investments and acquisitions, the analysts say. "Given strong expected cash generation and leverage at the middle of the target range, we also can envisage a further capital return of around 1 billion in buybacks," the Canadian bank adds. Shares closed at 117.05 pounds on Wednesday. ( najat.kantouar@wsj.com)

data.title: Gold Fields Reports Rise in Net Profit; Meets Revised Guidance
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

Gold Fields reported a rise in net profit for 2024 and said it met revised full-year guidance after a stronger second-half performance.

The diversified gold producer said net profit increased to $1.245 billion last year from $703.3 million in 2023.

The South African company said its results reflected the significant improvement in operational and financial performance in the second half of the year that helped it meet revised guidance for production volumes, all-in-sustaining costs and all-in-costs, it said.

Momentum has continued into the new year and it expects to deliver a marked operational and financial improvement in 2025, Gold Fields added.

All-in sustaining costs for 2024 increased 26% to $1,629 an ounce while all-in costs rose 24% to $1,873 an ounce.

The miner produced 2.07 million ounces of gold in 2024 compared with 2.30 million ounces the year before. In November, Gold Fields said it expected production for the year to be in the lower end of the 2.05 million-2.15 million ounces range it had guided for in August.

Gold Fields declared a final dividend of 7 rand (38 U.S. cents) a share, which took the total dividend for the year to 10 rand a share compared with 7.45 rand the year before.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: EFG International Negative Share Reaction Surprises — Market Talk
data.description: The drop in EFG International share drop after impressive results was surprising, Citi analysts say in a research note after the Swiss private bank's update on Wednesday. Shares opened higher and reverted course during the trading session to close down 2.7% at 13.96 Swiss francs. "Investors we spoke with were similarly surprised and in our view the most likely explanation is profit-taking on the back of strong share price appreciation (+20% over last three months), compounded by lack of liquidity," analysts write. They also point to the bank's acquisition of Cite Gestion which they say reflects EFG's constructive take on M&A. Compared with peers Julius Baer and Vontobel, which have done select deals for capabilities, EFG is the company most focused on acquiring for scale purposes, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

Saliran Eyes MYR32 Million Via IPO
data.description:
ACE-Market bound Saliran (KLSE:SALIRAN) is set to raise 32 million ringgit through its initial public offering, according to a Wednesday prospectus on Bursa Malaysia.

The IPO entails a public issue of 80.4 million shares and an offer for sale of 38.3 million shares at 0.27 ringgit apiece.

Proceeds will be used to establish a sales office in Indonesia, purchase of machinery, and repayment of bank borrowings, the industrial equipment supplier said.

data.title: Singapore Equities Appear to Be Less Volatile — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's Straits Times Index appears less volatile than its regional peers, says Carmen Lee, head of OCBC Investment Research, in a report. This makes the benchmark index a viable option for diversification, offering shelter against overall market fluctuations, she writes. The STI's valuations remain undemanding and are currently trading at a forward P/E ratio of 11.8X, which is 0.5 standard deviations below its 10-year historical average, she adds. OCBC maintains a positive outlook on the Singapore market, primarily due to its defensive characteristics and attractive dividend profile. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: South Korean shares snap seven-day rally as chip, automakers fall
data.description:
Key points:
  • KOSPI falls, foreigners net sellers
  • Korean won strengthens against dollar
  • South Korea benchmark bond yield rises
  • For the midday report, please clicknL2N3PB01R

Round-up of South Korean financial markets:

** South Korean shares snapped a seven-session rally on Thursday, as exporters of chips, automobiles and biopharmaceuticals fell amid uncertainty over U.S. tariffs.

** The benchmark KOSPI closed down 17.46 points, or 0.65%, at 2,654.06. On Wednesday, the index hit its highest point since late September 2024.

** U.S. President Donald Trump said he will announce tariffs related to lumber, cars, semiconductors and pharmaceuticals "over the next month or sooner".

** "There was profit-taking after recent gains as uncertainty remained over various issues including tariffs," said Seo Sang-young, an analyst at Mirae Asset Securities.

** Chipmaker Samsung Electronics fell 0.51%, while peer SK Hynix lost 2.97%.

** Hyundai Motor shed 0.49% and sister automaker Kia Corp lost 0.53%, while Samsung Biologics and biopharmaceutical peer Celltrion ended down 0.61% and 0.11%, respectively.

** Among other index heavyweights, battery manufacturers and steel producers rose, while e-commerce firms were mixed.

** Of the total 941 traded issues, 452 shares advanced, while 417 declined.

** Foreigners were net sellers of shares worth 271.9 billion won ($189.09 million).

** The won was quoted at 1,437.9 per dollar on the onshore settlement platform , 0.24% higher than its previous close at 1,441.4.

** In money and debt markets, March futures on three-year treasury bonds (KTBc1) rose 0.01 point to 106.64.

** The most liquid three-year Korean treasury bond yield (KR3YT=RR) rose by 0.4 basis points to 2.627%, while the benchmark 10-year yield rose by 2.4 bps to 2.893%.

($1 = 1,437.9600 won)

data.title: Correction: Zurich Insurance Posts Higher FY24 Net Income, Insurance Revenue
data.description:
(Corrects currency in second paragraph)

Zurich Insurance Group said Thursday that its net income and business operating profit for full-year 2024 increased year over year.

Net income after tax attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was $5.81 billion, compared with $4.35 billion a year ago. EPS moved to 35.33 francs from 26.71 francs.

The Swiss insurer's business operating profit rose to $7.75 billion from $7.38 billion.

Meanwhile, the board proposed a dividend of 28 francs per share for the period, subject to approval at an annual general meeting on April 9. It paid 26 francs per share a year ago.

Zurich Insurance anticipates a compound annual core EPS growth of above 9% between 2025 and 2027, compared with a previous target of 8% between 2023 and 2025.

data.title: Nikkei Falls 1.2%, Dragged by Auto, Chip Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks ended lower amid concerns about borrowing costs and continued U.S. tariffs. Auto and chip stocks led the declines. Subaru Corp. fell 4.1% and Kioxia Holdings dropped 3.9%. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.2% to 38678.04. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield rose 1 bp to 1.440%, the highest level since November 2009. Investors closely monitored developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies and their implications for Japanese businesses. USD/JPY was at 150.11, down from 151.47 as of Wednesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0649 GMT - Japanese stocks ended lower amid concerns about borrowing costs and continued U.S. tariffs. Auto and chip stocks led the declines. Subaru Corp. fell 4.1% and Kioxia Holdings dropped 3.9%. The Nikkei Stock Average fell 1.2% to 38678.04. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield rose 1 bp to 1.440%, the highest level since November 2009. Investors closely monitored developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies and their implications for Japanese businesses. USD/JPY was at 150.11, down from 151.47 as of Wednesday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0631 GMT - Zurich Insurance's print reflects the reliability of the Swiss group, Jefferies says after it published 2024 results largely in line with consensus expectations. "Looking on a line-by-line basis, we struggle to recall an occasion where results so closely matched consensus, which is perhaps the clearest indication that the group has become reassuringly reliable," analyst Philip Kett writes. He also highlights the 18 percentage point beat in its Swiss Solvency Test ratio thanks to more favorable financial markets and a benefit from the sale of its annuity book in Chile. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0614 GMT - Indonesia's banking sector may enter a period of tactical tailwinds, which could be a near-term catalyst for major banks, Nomura's Tushar Mohata and Verdhana Sekuritas's Erwin Wijaya say in a research report. A recent downtrend in blended rates on Bank Indonesia's rupiah securities to 6.442% may reduce arbitrage opportunities compared with the repo rate of 6.50%, the analysts say. This could provide scope for further reduction in issuance of such securities and for opportunities for Bank Indonesia's benchmark rate to continue to fall, they say. Major banks with more fixed-rate earnings assets have benefited more from potentially stable if not marginally falling funding costs, they say. Major lenders that could benefit include Bank Rakyat Indonesia and Bank Negara Indonesia, the analysts add. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0615 GMT - United Overseas Bank's share buyback reflects the management's confidence in the earnings outlook, Morningstar senior equity analyst Michael Makdad says in a note. The bank has guided for high-single-digit loan growth and double-digit fee growth this year, he writes. UOB's current and savings account ratio, has increased to 55% from 49% at the start of last year, he says. This gives the bank potential to defend its net interest margins even if the Fed cuts interest rates, a prospect that is looking less likely, he adds. Morningstar remains positive on UOB's 2025 earnings outlook and raises UOB's fair value estimate to S$41 from S$40. Shares are 0.1% lower at S$38.54.(amanda.lee@wsj.com)

0601 GMT - AI is likely to be the tech investment theme of the decade, one that offers significant opportunities in the years to come, the UBS Global Wealth Management Chief Investment Office says. For global markets excluding China, it forecasts that total AI spending will near $50 billon in 2026, which will be the fourth year since OpenAI rolled out ChatGPT, catalyzing the AI buzz. AI spending in China alone will likely be around $30 billon in 2028, while AI-related revenues should reach $50 billion, the fourth year since China's DeepSeek fueled enthusiasm about Chinese AI, it adds. Within China, the internet sector will outperform the broader market amid improving fundamentals, encouraging shareholder returns and macro policy support, it adds. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0507 GMT - Baidu is likely to benefit from strong AI business, say HSBC analysts in a commentary. The tech company's 4Q cloud revenue came in "meaningfully ahead of consensus" on stronger application programming interface demand, the analysts say. HSBC expects this growth momentum to sustain in 2025, with rising AI adoption from customers and cross-selling opportunities for AI infrastructure in the coming months. HSBC maintains a hold rating and raises target price of its ADRs slightly to US$94.00 from US$93.00. The ADRs last traded at US$88.03, while the Hong Kong-listed shares are down 2.7% at HK$85.95. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Statoil: Equinor ASA: Announcement of cash dividend per share in NOK for third quarter 2024
REG-Equinor ASA: Announcement of cash dividend per share in NOK for third quarter 2024
data.description:
Equinor ASA (OSE: EQNR, NYSE: EQNR) announced on 24 October 2024 an ordinary cash dividend per share of USD 0.35 and an extraordinary cash dividend per share of USD 0.35 for third quarter 2024.

The NOK cash dividend per share is based on average USDNOK fixing rate from Norges Bank in the period plus/minus three business days from record date 14 February 2025, in total seven business days.

Average Norges Bank fixing rate for this period was 11.1820. Total cash dividend for third quarter 2024 of USD 0.70 per share is consequently NOK 7.8274 per share.

On 28 February 2025, the cash dividend will be paid to relevant shareholders on Oslo Børs (Oslo Stock Exchange) and to holders of American Depositary Receipts ("ADRs") on New York Stock Exchange.

This information is published in accordance with the requirements of the Continuing Obligations and is subject to the disclosure requirements pursuant to section 5-12 of the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

data.title: French and Benelux stocks-Factors to watch
data.description:
Below are company-related news and stories from France and Benelux, which could have an impact on the region's markets or individual stocks.

ACCOR

Europe's largest hotel group by portfolio reported on Thursday annual room revenue growth slightly above market expectations, citing higher prices and occupancy rates.

AIRBUS

Europe's largest aerospace group predicted a 7% increase in commercial deliveries and confirmed a delay to its A350 freighter model as it posted lower core annual operating earnings broadly in line with expectations on Thursday.

ADP

The French airport operator confirmed on Wednesday its outlook for 2025.

CARREFOUR

Europe's largest food retailer said on Wednesday it would continue to lower prices this year as it reported a higher operating profit for its core French market and a strong cash flow.

COVIVIO

The French real estate company said on Wednesday it expects its recurring net result to come in at around 495 million euros ($515.10 million) at the end of 2025.

ERAMET

The mining group reported an annual core profit of 814 million euros on Wednesday and predicted higher gains for this year than in 2024.

RENAULT

The French automaker reported on Thursday an increase in operating profit last year, beating expectations. Separately, Renault's CFO told reporters on Thursday it was "very unlikely" that the company would return to Russia in the way envisioned at the time of its exit in 2022.

SCHNEIDER ELECTRIC

Schneider Electric forecast on Thursday a larger than expected increase in its 2025 profit margin, with sales beating market expectations in last year's fourth quarter boosted by strong data centre demand.

data.title: Cembra Posts Higher FY24 Net Income, Net Revenue
data.description:
Cembra Money Bank said Thursday its net income and net revenue for 2024 rose year over year.

Net income attributable to shareholders for the 12 months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was 170.4 million francs, compared with 158 million francs earlier. EPS moved to 5.80 francs from 5.38 francs.

The Swiss banking company's net revenue was 550.5 million francs, compared with 515.7 million francs a year ago.

Meanwhile, the board plans to propose a dividend of 4.25 francs per share for the period at the general meeting on April 24. It paid 4 francs per share a year ago.

For 2025, Cembra expects net revenue to rise at least in line with Swiss GDP growth and higher net income.

data.title: Keppel Liquidates Subsidiaries
data.description: Keppel completed the voluntary liquidation of its subsidiaries, KRE Portland, KRE Korea Dul, KRE Omaha and KRE Korea Hana, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Wednesday.

data.title: Schneider Electric CFO: Discrete Automation to Recover in 2H
Kingkey Financial International Enters Collaboration MoU for Green Energy Projects in Malaysia
data.description:
Kingkey Financial International has entered into a non-legally binding MoU to possible collaborate with Ideal Capital Berhad unit MUJUR SINARJAYA and WRISE Group, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Under the cooperation MoU, the parties will explore business opportunities through the financing and implementation of green energy projects and AI infrastructure with the Penang Technology Park in Malaysia.

The collaboration scope will potentially include the development of natural power plants, solar farms, and waste-to-energy facilities.

The Ideal Group specializes in buying and selling industrial land in Malaysia, including the Penang Technology Park, while WRISE Group is a wealth management firm.

data.description: Zinc increased to a 4-week high of 2907.00 USD/T. Over the past 4 weeks, Zinc lost 0.26%, and in the last 12 months, it increased 21.25%.

data.title: Schneider Electric Profit, Revenue Beat Market Views
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Schneider Electric posted a rise in full-year net profit and revenue, beating market views.

The French energy-management and automation group on Thursday said its net profit for 2024 rose to 4.27 billion euros ($4.45 billion) from 4.00 billion euros in the prior year.

Revenue rose 6.3% to 38.15 billion euros.

Analysts had forecast net profit of 4.12 billion euros and revenue of 37.65 billion euros, according to consensus estimates provided by the company.

Revenue for the energy management business rose 11.5% organic growth to 31.13 billion euros. The figure beat analysts' estimated 30.68 billion euros, according to the same consensus.

A progressive dividend of 3.90 euro per share will be proposed, up 11% on year, Schneider Electric said.

For 2025, the company targets adjusted Ebita organic growth between 10% and 15%, driven by 7% to 10% organic revenue growth and an organic adjusted Ebita margin improvement of between 50 and 80 basis points, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com
